channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Security and AI are topping business spend as companies look for effective boosts
item.description: Connectivity is proving vital for companies to get the most out of their security and artificial intelligence investments.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A social media ban for under-16s passes the Australian Senate and will soon be a world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Senate Thursday and will soon become a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 4 prerequisites for IT leaders to navigate today’s era of disruption
item.description: For its Generative AI Readiness Report, IT services company Avanade surveyed over 3,000 business and IT executives in 10 countries from companies with at least $500 million in annual revenue.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to use ref structs in C# 13
item.description: In the C# programming language, structs or structure types are value types that allow faster access because they are usually stored in the stack.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TradeMe’s bold new plan to boost number of women in technical leadership roles
item.description: New Zealand online marketplace TradeMe, has unveiled an ambitious plan to double the number of women in technical leadership roles, to address the persistent gender imbalance in the tech industry, where women remain underrepresented, especially in senior positions.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: American Airlines is expanding its airport ‘gate lice’ crackdown
item.description: American Airlines is cracking down on “gate lice” ahead of what’s expected to be a record-setting Thanksgiving travel season. The company is expanding new technology to more than 100 airports in hopes of keeping passengers from attempting to board a flight before their assigned group is called.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Coast Guard adds new cyber requirements for ship-to-shore cranes
item.description: The directive builds upon a mandate issued in February — the public notice doesn’t disclose specifics of the new requirements. The post Coast Guard adds new cyber requirements for ship-to-shore cranes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon Invests $4B in AI Startup, Rival to OpenAI: A Look at the Advancements and Challenges of Artificial Intelligence
item.description: Amazon invests billions in an AI startup rivaling OpenAI, while a Stanford professor is accused of using AI to fake testimony. We explore the advancements and challenges of artificial intelligence in this in-depth report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Northern lights may be faintly visible across parts of the US this Thanksgiving
item.description: Solar storms may produce faint auroras across the northern rim of the United States this Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ban on Australian Children Under 16 from Social Media: A Step Forward or a Wrong Turn?
item.description: Australia's move to ban children under 16 from social media sparks debate over safety, privacy, and the impact on young minds.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Majority of social media influencers share information without verifying its accuracy: UN report
item.description: The majority of social media influencers share information with their followers without verifying its accuracy, according to a new U.N. report that was released on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal investigators in New York are seeking records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed this summer in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Shining a light on shadow AI: Three ways to keep your enterprise safe
item.description: To mitigate shadow AI, leaders should build cross-functional teams to leverage interdisciplinary expertise and foster organizational awareness. The post Shining a light on shadow AI: Three ways to keep your enterprise safe first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: How to (kindly) ask people not to post your kids' photos on social media
item.description: With kids facing a seemingly increasing amount of threats from creeps on the internet, here's how you can respectfully ask people not to post photos of your children on social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration finalizes nearly $8B in CHIPS funding for Intel
item.description: Intel will receive about $7.86 billion in funding from the CHIPS and Science Act to build new semiconductor manufacturing facilities across four states under its finalized agreement with the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Lauren Boebert Cameo page disappears amid House ethics concerns
item.description: Rep. Lauren Boebert (R-Colo.) briefly offered videos on Cameo, a website on which public figures sell personalized videos to fans, before the page went offline amid questions about whether it would have violated House ethics rules.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, The Hill

item.title: Interpol clamps down on cybercrime and arrests over 1,000 suspects in Africa
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Interpol arrested 1,006 suspects in Africa during a massive two-month operation, clamping down on cybercrime that left tens of thousands of victims, including some who were trafficked, and produced millions in financial damages, the global police organization said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google, DOJ wrap up second antitrust trial
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) and Google wrapped up the tech giant’s second antitrust trial in two years Monday, as the company seeks to fend off monopoly allegations in the advertising technology space. After a relatively brief two-week trial in September, the two sides reconvened in U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: NARA to launch AI tool to improve customer experience
item.description: Sheena Burrell, who was NARA's chief information officer until Nov. 21, said the agency is upgrading systems to deliver a modernized user experience. The post NARA to launch AI tool to improve customer experience first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Elon Musk entertains idea of buying MSNBC
item.description: Elon Musk is flirting with the idea of buying MSNBC amid speculation the left-leaning cable news network could be put up for sale.
item.dc:creator: Brian Niemietz, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel’s CHIPS Act grant reduced as production delays and losses mount
item.description: The US government has scaled back Intel’s preliminary CHIPS Act grant from $8.5 billion to under $8 billion, reflecting concerns over the company’s delayed investments and financial woes, The New York Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Dell adds to PowerEdge server lineup
item.description: Dell Technologies introduced new hardware products and services at two separate conferences, the Supercomputing 24 show in Atlanta and Microsoft’s Ignite conference.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 3 data engineering trends riding Kafka, Flink, and Iceberg
item.description: Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, and Apache Iceberg are among the most popular technologies in the data ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Roblox gives parents more control over kids' experience
item.description: Roblox, which has been downloaded millions of times, announced sweeping and restrictive settings for minor safety.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4
